10101.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10101.TaskInfo: A foreboding dream-the world in ruins. A vision of the past, or a warning of the future?

10102.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10102.TaskInfo:
You awaken from a chaotic dream to find yourself in an unfamiliar city, where a strange girl named Airona claims she saved your life and even paid for your lodgings. But you've lost your memory—and your money. To repay your debt, you form a duo with Airona and the Luno Partners and set off to earn your keep.
You arrive in Asterleeds just in time for the Seabreeze Festival. At the Festival, you dominate every competition, winning champion in all events and raking in rewards with Airona. Your stunning performance draws the attention of runner-up Narulu, who offers clues to the "Tales of the Plains."

10103.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10103.TaskInfo:
While collecting the horns of Caprahorns on the plains, you and Airona rescue a merchant from black mist monsters-thanks to the intervention of a Snow Knight named Tina. Introduced by Airona, Tina joins your party. But the monsters keep coming back even after defeat.
Airona suspects the Void Engram—a dreadful phenomenon that devours life and warps the mind—is to blame. To uncover the truth, you delve deeper into the Void Engram. Fighting off wave after wave of Void Corruption monsters, you and your allies push forward and emerge victorious.

10104.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10104.PhaseName: Nothing New Beneath the Void Engram
10104.TaskInfo:
Your investigation takes you to the Cliff Ruins, where signs point to a deliberate plot behind the disasters. There, the Void Engram overwhelms you, but Airona and Tina fortunately rush to your rescue.
You finally confront the Man in Black responsible for spreading the Void Engram. The Man in Black aims to unleash more of it, but your team defeats him. Though the Man in Black vanishes with the Void Engram, the failure is clear: the Tales of the Plains is resolved, and the curse has lifted.
You and Airona return to the Pioneer Bureau to claim your reward, only to find that Airona's past expenses will be deducted from your bounty. The remaining sum is entrusted to you.
Guild Officer Valeria then invites you to officially join the Pioneer Bureau, marking the start of your new life as an adventurer.

10105.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10105.PhaseName: The Phantom Thief and the Forbidden Journal
10105.TaskInfo:
To pay off your remaining debt, you and Airona accept a secret commission: uncover the true identity of the Thief Snow Knight and recover a stolen journal. Airona suspects Tina may be involved because she once wore the Snow Knight suit, though the suit had been rented from a stall at the Seabreeze Festival. Before Tina rented the suit, it had been rented by a thug named Abeku. You track him down, defeat him, and force him to confess. He'd been hired by a mysterious backer to steal what he thought was a diary, but it turns out to be Tina's classified file! Realizing the danger, Tina decides to handle the matter herself. She leaves you and Airona with a reward, but Airona, feeling she let Tina down, grows quiet. The two of you go for a walk to lift her spirits.
As you stroll through the shops, messages from merchants summon you to Arena Town. There, you discover the true client of the commission was none other than Commander Olvera. With the file recovered, she apologizes and gives you both a voucher for The Spinning Rudder.

10106.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10106.TaskInfo:
You enjoy your time at The Spinning Rudder, only for Narulu to bring word of the Fireworks Festival. Airona excitedly drags you along, but en route, you encounter the Man in Black again. The Man in Black reveals that he was behind Abeku's theft and wants Tina's file for himself. Claiming he has a "gift" for Tina related to the festival, he attempts to trap you in Void Engram but is shocked to see you unaffected. When you reject his offer to join him, he leaves frustrated.
You shake off the Void Engram and rush to find Airona. The two of you race to the festival site where Tina suddenly erupts in Void Engram while accepting a medal. Chaos ensues. Airona dashes off to find Tina. You stay behind to help the Asterleeds Defense Corps rescue civilians and destroy the Void Prisms. Thanks to your efforts, the people are saved.

10107.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10107.TaskInfo: At the ancient ruins, you reunite with your companions to face the Man in Black who attacked Tina. His obsession with you runs deep—deep enough that, mid-battle, he forcefully separates Airona and Olvera just to sway you again. But he disappears once more, leaving Tina in an even more dire state. While caring for her, you're suddenly drawn into her mind. Within that fragile realm, you glimpse the sorrow she's hidden beneath her smile. Guided by you, Tina confronts the shadows of her past and finds the strength to overcome them. She awakens, saved and whole again.

10108.TitleName: Episode I: Beginning of the Journey
10108.TaskInfo:
After leaving Tina's Mindrealm, you sleep at the tavern for 3 days. Airona tells you that your body holds a Resonance Power capable of stabilizing lives eroded by Void Engram. It probably has something to do with your forgotten past. It was thanks to this mysterious power that you were able to save Tina. Now, with the Fireworks Festival about to begin, Airona invites you to go out and enjoy it with her.
At twilight, you head to the beach to meet her and enjoy the Fireworks Festival together. Beneath the stars and fireworks, you make a promise for the future.

10201.TitleName: Episode II: The Light and Shadow
10201.TaskInfo: After the fireworks, you return to The Double-Faced Coin to find a curious Box waiting for you—inside, a Medal Shard. The moment you touch it, your body is seized by Void Engram, and the witch Hruga takes control. She is the true mastermind, the Man in Black is her pawn, and the recent string of disasters, from the Tales of the Plains to the stolen file and the opening ceremony explosion, were all aimed at Tina, to break the Seal that binds Hruga. Your companions arrive just in time to pull you back. Council Chair Lamond then appears, entrusting you and Airona with a new commission: uncover the truth behind the Void Engram-infused medal. In return, he promises to clear Airona's debt, and to provide resources for the journey ahead. Thus begins a new chapter in your quest.

10202.TitleName: Episode II: The Light and Shadow
10202.TaskInfo:
You and Airona report to the Pioneer Bureau and set off for the Artisan Guild. Guildmaster Dorothy shares everything he knows about the medal—its forging, storage, and those who handled it. The signs point to tampering during its time in storage. Your investigation uncovers suspicious clues: a pair of gloves marked with a strange residue. The gloves belong to a man named Ryan, and in his locker, you find a confession. He acted under coercion from the Dark Mist Legion.
Olvera joins you to track him down. In the Ancient City of Vill, you find Ryan cornered by remnants of the Dark Mist Legion. After driving them off, Olvera questions him and decides to bring him back for further investigation. You and Airona return to Asterleeds, deliver your report to Lamond, and receive a generous reward. The job is done, for now, at least. It's time for some well-earned rest.

10203.TitleName: Episode II: The Light and Shadow
10203.TaskInfo:
During your downtime, you and Airona spot a mysterious ship, the Wavecrest Carnival, anchored along the coast. Narulu says it's been chartered by a Byrnhald tycoon and is hosting a free party. You board with an invitation, only to sense something strange aboard. When Narulu's kitten is taken by "tycoon" Kakater, Airona grows suspicious. Alongside Tina and the others, you dig up damning evidence: Kakater is a fraud trying to launder wealth through a double. Back at the card table, you present your findings and expose him. That's when the real tycoon, Taltraud, steps out. Kakater attempts to blow up the ship and escape, but everyone makes it out safely.
On shore, he is captured and revealed to be the notorious swindler known as "Greenbottle Fly." Taltraud then reveals his true identity, a Special Executive Officer from Byrnhald, on a covert mission to eliminate the Dark Mist Legion. Tina suggests reporting the discovery to Council Chair Lamond. The group travels to Ocean Hill, where Lamond recognizes Taltraud and agrees to cooperate on "Operation White Night." Taltraud requests access to the reconnaissance records of Asterleeds, and your team heads to the archives.
There, you uncover clues pointing to the Deputy Squad Leader, Jayden, who vanished in the Towering Ruin after a failed infiltration attempt during the Asterleeds Independence War. Taltraud believes Jayden may hold a key, something known as a "Imagine Seed," that could be used against Denvel. The party sets out once again, to the Towering Ruin.

10204.TitleName: Episode II: The Light and Shadow
10204.TaskInfo: Your journey to the Towering Ruin begins. Airona parts ways to aid the wounded at the Andra Resting Post, while Tina sets out to scout the surrounding terrain. Tatta promises to meet you at the Ruins. On the way, you and Olvera are ambushed by the Dark Mist Legion. Tatta draws the attackers away, buying you time. Guided by Sea Lily markers, you reunite with Tina and press deeper into the Ruins. There, you uncover the elusive Imagine Seed, but are swiftly ambushed by Denvel and the Squad. In a desperate move, you use the Imagine Seed to briefly bring Denvel back to his senses. However, the witch, Hruga, reasserts her grip, and Tatta is seized in the chaos.

10205.TitleName: Episode II: The Light and Shadow
10205.TaskInfo:
You and Tina trace the Dark Mist Legion to their fortress and find Tatta imprisoned in the dungeon. There, you uncover a chilling truth: Denvel is conspiring with the Man in Black and holds the Hruga's Horn. They're preparing to launch an all-out assault on Asterleeds. While evading Denvel, you're nearly caught until Tatta intervenes by attracting Denvel's attention.
After a narrow escape, you regroup outside the Dark Mist Fortress. At the campsite, Tina asks you to return to Asterleeds and warn Council Chair Lamond of Denvel's plan. She and Tatta will continue the pursuit. The fate of Asterleeds hangs in the balance.

10301.TaskInfo: After escaping the Dark Mist Fortress, you return to Ocean Hill and report Denvel's looming assault to Council Chair Lamond. Understanding the weight of your burden, he offers you an envelope containing intel on the Corps and a ticket to leave Asterleeds. The choice is yours. At the gate, Narulu spots your somber expression and invites you for a walk by the bridge. His gentle encouragement helps clear your mind. Moved by his sincerity, you hand him the ticket, urging him to seek safety instead. Narulu, reminded of loved ones lost to war, promises not to vanish from your life and then leaves. With renewed purpose, you open the report and learn that the Dark Mist Legion has attacked the Bapharian Guard Post. Without hesitation, you rush to the front.

10302.TaskInfo:
By the time you reach the Creed's camp, it's already ablaze—beyond saving. Amid the devastation, you reconnect with Olvera and share what you've learned. Soon, word arrives: the Dark Mist Legion is headed for the Andra Resting Post, where Airona is stationed. Without delay, you and Olvera rush to her aid.
The Andra Resting Post is sheltering those who were affected by the Void Engram during the Festival. Airona is working tirelessly to heal them. Together with Olvera, you repel the attackers and rescue a priest named Stanley. He carries an ancient artifact, the Engram Collector, capable of cleansing those consumed by Void Corruption. Thanks to your efforts, the afflicted are cured. But amid the relief, a Dark Mist Trooper reveals himself and shouts a warning. In that moment, the plains twist and ripple, and inexplicable phenomena erupt near Minsterhorn and the Statue of the Hero...

10303.TaskInfo: Minsterhorn lies in shambles when you arrive, its defenders devastated. Amid the wreckage, Narulu is shielding the villagers. Fighting side by side, you drive back the monsters. Narulu explains that Denvel had used the Hruga's Horn to summon a deadly storm and unleash the monsters now tearing through the village. You plan to investigate further and find a way to stop the lightning storm. Though Narulu offers to join you, you ask him to report back to Olvera in Asterleeds instead. But Narulu secretly follows you, and you have no choice but to let him come along. Following his earlier notes, you crack the Lightning Barrier and press on. Soon, Airona contacts you with a grim revelation: the Engram Anomaly stems from a disruption in the star energy flow. There's a Lightning Storm Core at the heart of the chaos. Destroy it, and the disaster ends. But it's guarded by something powerful. As you near the core, you ask Narulu to take cover inside a house. Alone, you confront a monstrous Tempest Ogre. The fight is fierce, but you prevail. Just as you strike down the beast, the Lightning Storm Core unleashes a catastrophic bolt of lightning. Narulu rushes in to shield you—at the cost of his life. When the storm clears, a Void Sigil marks where the Core once was. It turns out this was all Denvel's doing. You set off for the Statue to meet Airona.

10304.TaskInfo: At the Statue of the Hero, Airona has already dispelled the Engram Anomaly. You share what happened at Minsterhorn, and together you mourn Narulu's sacrifice. A Void Sigil stains the Statue—just like the one you saw before in Minsterhorn. Airona concludes that Denvel is using the Hruga's Horn to plant them. These Void Sigils sever the flow of Engram, birthing further disasters that could doom the plains. But for now, she has no way to erase them. Seeking answers, the two of you head to the forest warden's cabin. As you approach, you realize this was Narulu's home. The weight of grief deepens. You follow his kitten through the woods and find the warden couple under attack. You and Airona drive the monsters off and rescue them. From their account, it was the Dark Mist Legion that unleashed this catastrophe. They mention Narulu went to Minsterhorn but hasn't returned. Their concern and the kitten's plaintive mews, pierce your heart. You hesitate. Should you tell them the truth? After parting with the couple, Airona sings an ancient hymn to honor Narulu's memory.

10305.TaskInfo:
With the storms momentarily calmed, the team agrees to meet at the Andra Resting Post.
Tina reaches out—she and Tatta have tracked Denvel down. You, Olvera, and Airona quickly divide the roles: Olvera will lead a surprise attack, Airona will stay behind to study the Void Sigils, and you'll link up with Tina and Tatta.
In a secluded part of the plains, you regroup with them and hear the plan: Tatta will act as bait to distract Denvel's elite guard, while Tina sneaks into the camp to use the Legacy. Being concerned for Tina's safety, you volunteer to take on her part of the mission. The three of you approach the enemy camp, but Denvel sees it coming. Just as he strikes, Tatta blocks Denvel's attack to shield you. Denvel spares you and withdraws. Afterward, Tatta opens up about her connection with Denvel, shedding light on long-buried secrets.
You escort her back to Asterleeds. Soon, Airona's message reaches you, and the group reconvenes at the outpost.

10306.TaskInfo: Airona devises a way to break the Void Sigils, but it hinges on finding a rare source of pure, potent Engram somewhere on the plains. You and Tina head out to search—your path eventually leading to the shadowy depths of the Andra Tunnels. There, you encounter the unstable priest Stanley and a few Dark Mist Legion Deserters. All have been poisoned by the noxious gas seeping through the tunnels and are on the verge of death. The Engram you're after lies in a legacy once safeguarded by Stanley. But the priest, consumed by despair and disillusioned with humanity, shatters the legacy and releases the Engram, intending to perish alongside the others. In an almost impossible situation, you and Tina throw caution aside, risking your lives to pull everyone to safety. Deeply moved by your selflessness, Stanley has a change of heart. He offers you a new lead—a place where pure Engram might still exist: the Kanamia Tribe. And so, you and Tina set off once more...toward the land of the Kanamia Tribe.

10401.TaskInfo: Following the priest's guidance, you and your companions arrive at the Kanamia Tribe. Someone left behind a strange letter. Following its instructions, you each set out to gather intel separately. Beneath the moonlit sky, you cross paths with a mysterious girl named Rorola, the sender of the letter. She reveals that the Kanamia Tribe's Forbidden Zone is tied to the fate of the entire plain. To earn the right to enter the Forbidden Zone, you join the Kanamia Brave Trial, hunting the fearsome Iron Fang in the dense forest and surviving a brutal gauntlet. Though you emerge victorious, the Chieftain sees through your outsider identity. At the critical moment, Rorola appears to defend you, revealing that a dire omen looms over the tribe and that you are the prophesied one destined to change its fate.

10402.TaskInfo:
The first thing you do after becoming the Kanamia Brave is to find the key that unlocks the gate to the Forbidden Zone. However, the key is hidden within a mysterious Wander Site, home to the spirits. With Rorola's help, you travel there and encounter a strange spirit named Ted.
With Ted's assistance, you learn about the past between Kanamia and Asterleeds, including the war and those who were lost. The key to the Forbidden Zone lies with the fallen soldier, Yaretsi...

10403.TaskInfo: After successfully obtaining the key to the Forbidden Zone and leaving the Wander Site, you discover that the Kanamia Tribe is facing a new crisis. Another Engram Anomaly has appeared, and violent storms sweep through the tribe. The chieftain and Rorola are caught in the storm, and in the critical moment, you and your companions step up...

10404.TaskInfo:
With the storm quelled, you are now respected by the Kanamians as the new Kanamia Brave. The gate to the Forbidden Zone finally opens, and you and your companions retrieve the Divine Legacy imbued with pure Engram, the Engram Collector. With the relic in hand, everyone sets off to prepare a counterattack. Upon coming into contact with the Engram Strength, you regain fragments of your lost memories.
At that moment, your fate and that of this continent become deeply entwined once again.

10501.TitleName: Episode V: Countdown to the End
10501.TaskInfo:
Everyone prepares to use the Engram Collector to resolve the Void Sigils. The group starts with the sigil at the Dragon Claw Valley, only to discover that its spread was caused by Denvel's forbidden art. Still, you activate the collector and successfully suppress the spread. Afterward, Olvera and Tina return to the city to deal with the crisis in Asterleeds while you and Airona head to Minsterhorn to handle the remaining sigils. At Minsterhorn and the Statue of the Hero, you manage to suppress two more sigils and encounter a young girl who stayed to protect Narulu. You then receive a letter from Agnes. Upon learning that Asterleeds is under attack from monsters, you and Airona rush back to the city.
Back in Asterleeds, you help Jerrad fight off the monsters, rescue civilians, and discuss strategies to deal with Denvel. Council Chair Lamond suggests Tatta might have some clues, and the group asks you to find him.

10502.TitleName: Episode V: Countdown to the End
10502.TaskInfo:
To address the crisis, you first visit the Pioneer Bureau to check on Tatta but learn that he has gone missing. You and Tina follow traces of fire and eventually find him. Tatta tells everyone that the turmoil in Asterleeds is likely a distraction created by Denvel to set up the final sigil. Then, he leaves alone to investigate Dark Mist Fortress.
Using Tatta's information, you deduce that the final Void Sigil may be at the Shrine entrance. You then go to the Artisan Guild to help the embattled Airona and Dorothy fend off Void Monsters. You learn that the Engram Collector must be brought near the Shrine.
You later reunite with Olvera and catch up with Airona and Tina en route to the Shrine. Just before arrival, a massive transformation occurs in the city as Denvel activates the final Void Sigil. The Engram Collector is destroyed, and you absorb the scattered Engram, becoming a vessel for it. Though this grants you immense power, it puts your body at extreme risk. Despite everyone's concern, you decide to go confront Denvel. Your companions stand by you, protecting you on the way.
Olvera stays behind to hold off Dark Mist Legion pursuers. Dark Mist Legion takes hostages, and Tina gets surrounded when attempting to rescue hostages. You and Airona continue the trip. At the Shrine, Airona stays to fight off more enemies, and you finally confront Denvel. You channel all the Engram to purify the Void Sigils across the plains, shattering Denvel's plot.

10503.TitleName: Episode V: Countdown to the End
10503.TaskInfo:
Though injured in the battle, Denvel remains arrogant. Tatta appears and tries to stop Denvel but is attacked. Later, he departs alone to prepare for a final clash with the Dark Mist Legion. The group decides to strike back while the Void Sigils are purified and the Dark Mist Fortress' defenses are down. Tina and Olvera prepare for battle, while you and Airona are tasked with informing the citizens.
At the Town Gate Square, the public enthusiastically responds to the planned attack against Dark Mist Fortress. You speak with soldiers, adventurers, and artisans, learning their thoughts and determination. Then, you head to rejoin your companions and prepare for the offensive.

10504.TitleName: Episode V: Countdown to the End
10504.TaskInfo:
After a bitter struggle in Dark Mist Fortress, the group finally defeats Denvel. But the Man in Black appears again, steals Hruga's Horn, and detonates Void Engram inside the bastion. In this desperate moment, Tina uses the Core of Stellarium to save everyone at the cost of her own life. Only your Resonance Power can save her. You bring Tina to the Engram Hub and revive her. However, the Core of Stellarium is gone for good.
Back in the city, you visit The Double-Faced Coin, speak with Jerrad, Valeria, and Dorothy to gather updates, and then return to your room to rest. In your dreams, some of your memories return. Upon waking, you talk to Airona and learn that your memories may be connected to the source of the Void Engram. Also, Airona has found out that Hruga's Horn is in the Bahamar Highlands.
Before leaving Asterleeds, you say farewell to Olvera and Tina. At the city gate of Asterleeds, you and Airona prepare to embark on your journey to the highlands. The story of the seabreeze comes to a pause for now, and a new adventure awaits.

10601.TaskInfo:
You and Airona arrive in the Bahamar Highlands. The Pillar of Divinity stands majestically before you, but the treacherous scent of Illusory Blooms lurks underfoot. In a flash, you are dragged into a vision filled with gunpowder and whispers. Fortunately, a young girl named Fafala's desperate assistance allows you both to escape. Afterward, the Priest Lothar steps in to purify the seeds, but even he seems to struggle against the power of the Illusory Blooms.
Later, you and Airona find a moment's respite at Fafala's home, where you taste the legendary Rainbean Rice and Highland Orange juice, only to discover their taste is a trial of courage in itself. Amid the laughter, you are gifted a protective talisman called a Starsoul Flask and hear strange rumors of frequent Illusory Plagues and a peculiar traveling troupe. Airona keenly senses that the highlands' Engram disturbance is converging toward the northeast—that the mine belonging to the Duchy of Byrnhald may hold clues about Hruga's Horn. With these unsolved mysteries in mind, you and she prepare to depart on an even more dangerous journey.

10602.TaskInfo:
You and Airona hit a wall at the mine checkpoint. Denied passage, your only recourse is to gather information from the villagers. Along the way, you save a little girl, Lily, from being knocked over by a soldier, and through this, you meet Village Chief Alphonse and the other villagers.
The mine's lockdown and the heavy guard by the Blackstone Legion make the main road impassable, so you head to the Pillar of Divinity to see what you can find out. The pilgrims here babble on about strange tales and miracles, but no one can give a straight answer. Instead, amid the chaos, a young woman named Darya seizes the opportunity to steal your and Airona's Starsoul Flasks. After a chase and confrontation, she admits she knows the secret entrance to the mine. She even reveals that the underground is also infested with Illusory Blooms, and the miners are suffering terribly under oppression. She proposes a deal: she will lead you to infiltrate the mine in exchange for you not pursuing the theft.
Faced with this cunning Miss Thief, you and Airona must decide whether to accept this risky bargain...

10603.PhaseName: Where the Sunlight Does Not Reach
10603.TaskInfo:
Under Darya's guidance, you and Airona bypass the checkpoint and infiltrate the Sislesa Mine through a hidden secret passage. After making your way through the pitch-black mine tunnel, you finally catch a glimpse of the sorting area, strictly controlled by the Blackstone Legion, and those abnormally radiant red, Engram-rich ores.
Following the clues, you witness an even more disturbing reality: The miners have lost their minds due to the Illusory Blooms, and the Starsoul Flasks provide at best a fleeting moment of calm. At that moment, the squad launches a surprise raid to arrest people. Even the weak Navi is dragged away. You have no choice but to first hide to avoid the search, then wait for an opportunity to rescue your companion. To stop the calamity, you confront the Illusory Bloom head-on in the mine tunnel, resonating with the Stamen Crystal to shatter the illusion and collecting a new crystal.
Filled with frustration and self-blame, Darya resolves to continue leading the way. And so, you turn your attention to the most heavily guarded mining cave...

10604.PhaseName: A Path of Survival for the Brave
10604.TaskInfo:
On the way to the Mining Zone, you and Airona accidentally find a lost Blackstone Legion badge, sensing from it the tense atmosphere of an impending storm. Darya suddenly raises the stakes, demanding five hundred Starsoul Flasks as her condition and revealing that she may have a relative who was lost in the mine. This personal stake and urgency make the atmosphere even more complex. You overhear that the squad intends to lower the isolation gate and release sleeping gas.
With the way back completely sealed, you have no choice but to risk passing through the Rock Serpent's lair. Tremors and collapses accompany your journey as danger closes in, until the massive Rock Serpent bursts from the ground, forcing you all to fight your way out.
In the end, you finally manage to leave the mine from the other side, able to catch your breath for a moment. Darya decides to go back to investigate for more information, promising to pass on what she finds, while you and Airona plan to head to the Pillar of Divinity to find a way to obtain Starsoul Flasks, preparing to re-enter the mining cave.

10701.TitleName: Episode II: Where All Fervor Sleeps
10701.TaskInfo:
After Darya's hasty departure, you and Airona decide to find a way to procure the Starsoul Flasks. The two of you first scout the area around the Pillar of Divinity for information, deducing that the person in charge of distribution, Boyce, is on duty. You then go to negotiate with him.
Using what you saw in the mine and the crystals extracted from the Illusory Blooms as evidence, you persuade Boyce, who offers to join your team into the mine and help coordinate supplies, but only after the necessary materials are restocked. You immediately collect Rockthistle Seeds and return with Boyce to meet Lothar. After confirming the situation, the priest decides to go to the Commander to negotiate in person, while also arranging for aid to be provided in bulk. And so, the five hundred Starsoul Flasks and accompanying supplies are ready, with Boyce as escort.
Airona continues to investigate the remnants of the Illusory Blooms, while you prepare to infiltrate the mine again, aiming for the mining cave and the root of the Engram disorder.

10702.TitleName: Episode II: Where All Fervor Sleeps
10702.TaskInfo:
You reunite with Airona on the outskirts of the mine, confirming that the giant Illusory Bloom has been reduced to an empty shell. You then meet up with Boyce, who has brought the Starsoul Flasks. Darya left a cryptic and hastily scrawled letter, suggesting that you disguise yourselves as merchants to blend in at the Bekmar Transport Station. To obtain the necessary disguises and a pass, you organize goods for a merchant in exchange for a share of his caravan's travel permit. The three of you successfully infiltrate the Transport Station but are quickly suspected due to your identities. Only after showing the Starsoul Flasks as cargo do you gain the approval of the leader, Maire. She lends you a Special Permit, but on the condition that you identify the traitor in her crew.
Following the clues, you first discover that Hebard has been allowing medicine to flow to the miners, then you uncover that Dedrick has been secretly selling rockthistle seed tea to the soldiers.
Finally, you confront Nevelle, who is carrying Crimson Marrow, and fight off the Blackstone Legion soldiers he summons. The evidence left after the battle points to a deeper conspiracy: Nevelle, instigated by a person in black, is colluding with the squad, intending to replace Maire and smuggle Crimson Marrow. You present your findings to Maire, who confirms that the person in black has been moving between the major caravans and the squad, constantly stirring up trouble.
As the situation becomes more complex, you take the Special Permit and prepare to find Boyce, who has wandered off to clear his head, before heading to the Mustard Tavern together to continue investigating the disorder in the mine and the true whereabouts of Hruga's Horn.

10703.TitleName: Episode II: Where All Fervor Sleeps
10703.PhaseName: The Astral Stones Speak in Silence
10703.TaskInfo: You and Airona regroup outside the mine. Boyce confesses that he has been secretly listening in and has noticed that the squad's patrols have become abnormally tense. Meanwhile, Darya keeps her promise and brings a lead on the miners' leader, Grieg. At your first meeting, Grieg refuses to take strangers into the mining cave. But soon after, the miners are attacked by monsters, and you step forward to save those who are trapped. Andre tragically sacrifices himself to save his comrades. Following the miners' custom, you select an Astral Stone for him, leaving his memory among the stone pile. Because of this, Grieg lets down his guard and confesses that the squad is in complete disarray, and that their mining tasks and the oppression they face are constantly intensifying. Although he still worries that a rash move could endanger his fellow miners, with his brothers' support, he decides to help you. He suggests first gathering intelligence at the Mustard Tavern on the timing of the squad's searches to find an opportunity to enter the mining cave.

10704.TitleName: Episode II: Where All Fervor Sleeps
10704.PhaseName: The Shadow Puts on a New Face
10704.TaskInfo:
With Grieg's help, you and your companions infiltrate the Mining Zone and personally witness the Blackstone Legion secretly refining and transporting Crimson Marrow. Airona deduces from this that the mastermind is not a small faction but the squad's high command, and that the person in black has very likely been colluding with them all along.
The gravity of the situation makes the purpose of the investigation even clearer—this batch of ore must be prevented from falling into the hands of the person in black. However, the situation is becoming increasingly urgent. The search party is closing in, and you have no choice but to take Darya and retreat quickly through the secret passage. At this very moment, a mysterious figure appears, making the truth even more elusive...

10801.TitleName: Episode III: The Buried and the Broken
10801.TaskInfo:
You and your companions piece together clues from the shift change schedule and confirm that a mutiny occurred at the mine during an undocumented period. The duty rules were quietly rewritten, manpower was drastically reduced, and the whereabouts of sergeants such as Milo, Breck, and Devin are now a mystery. You first try to get the truth from Milo, who has long been corrupted by his will to survive. Then, following the clues, you find Breck, who, despite appearing to go with the flow, has left behind a key to the mine caves.
Inside the mine cave, you find an anonymous journal and learn that Crimson Marrow has been mined under the Grand Duke's orders for at least a year, and that someone named Klaus was caught up in the storm for his loyalty to those orders. The clues ultimately point to Devin—only by finding this sergeant, who still supports the commander, can the fragments of the truth be pieced together, blocking the outflow of ore. Darya returns to the transfer zone first to look after the miners, while you, Airona, and Boyce follow the mine cart tracks to the Transport Station, ready to continue digging into the smuggling chain and the mastermind behind it.

10802.TitleName: Episode III: The Buried and the Broken
10802.PhaseName: The Blind Spot at the Exit
10802.TaskInfo: You, Airona, and Boyce infiltrate the Transport Station to investigate and unexpectedly discover a hidden diversion at the smelting outlet. The Crimson Marrow is being quietly intercepted and sent back to the Mining Zone. By resonating with a Nappo, you retrieve the fragments, proving that the so-called "output rates" and "waste" are just a front. Tailing Reiner, you witness firsthand how he manipulates the mine under the pretext of lowering output rates, treating the miners as expendable labor. The clues gradually point to his collusion with the person in black. To stop the outflow of ore, you and Airona decide to go deeper underground to uncover the truth, while Boyce heads back to look after Darya and the miners. And so, you part ways to take on your new tasks.

10803.TitleName: Episode III: The Buried and the Broken
10803.PhaseName: The Wind and Ashes Took the Answers
10803.TaskInfo: You and your companions reunite in the transfer zone, only to witness Grieg and the miners being brutally threatened by Reiner. Taylor falls, an innocent victim, and a mixture of fear and anger fills every heart. Darya, devastated by her compatriot's betrayal, is on the verge of collapse and insists on finding a more drastic way to change their fate. Boyce, however, insists on reporting the situation to the Duchy, leading to a complete break between them. In his grief, Grieg decides to lead the miners in a desperate breakout, while Airona proposes another option—for you to return to Asterleeds first to seek help. You are forced to set off in a new direction alone, while the crisis in the mine behind you continues to deepen.

10804.TitleName: Episode III: The Buried and the Broken
10804.PhaseName: The Seabreeze Pushes Open a Secret Door
10804.TaskInfo: You return to Asterleeds with news from the mine, attempting to seek aid. Chairman Lamond, caught between caution and powerlessness, hesitates to take action, able only to hope for a turn for the better in the future. Dejected, you reunite with Jerrad in the bustling Central District, only to find that this carefree old friend has secretly been planning an operation. He masks his seriousness with jokes, disguising one hundred Asterleeds Resistance Corps soldiers as adventurers, preparing to head to the highlands to provide support in secret. Along the way, you work together to foil the plot of a spy known as "Blackbird," confirming that your plan has not been exposed. Back on the street, you accept a warm steamed bun from an ordinary old woman, as if receiving an answer and strength from the city itself. Finally, amidst the intertwining peace and bustle, you get ready to set off once more, your gaze fixed on the distant highlands.

10901.TitleName: Episode IV: Giving Meaning to Every Bloom
10901.PhaseName: Sadness Spreads to Every Pair of Eyes
10901.TaskInfo:
You return to the Bahamar Highlands with Jerrad, only to find Lallipar enveloped by Illusory Blooms. The familiar streets and fields have lost their former vitality. Fafala is struggling to hold on. Airona is here investigating the ore trade. You stand side-by-side once more, seeking a way out of the villagers' plight. Jerrad, using the alias "Rab," negotiates and promises to send aid and supplies to the mine, while Alphonse and the Creed struggle to weigh their options, caught between the Illusory Plague and a shortage of supplies. You witness the quarrels and divisions among the pilgrims, and you hear Lothar and Alphonse confront the truth of the Starsoul Flasks—the comfort they bring is not true salvation.
The Illusory Blooms erupt once more. Federico returns, wounded, with a warning that a greater calamity is approaching. Fear and hope intertwine, the village is in turmoil, and you must once again step forward, following the remaining clues to find the giant Illusory Bloom that threatens the entire highlands.

10902.TitleName: Episode IV: Giving Meaning to Every Bloom
10902.PhaseName: Moonlight Ignites a Flame in the Heart
10902.TaskInfo: You pursue Nappo to the edge of the highlands, where, nourished by the Illusory Blooms, it has grown massive and dangerous. After a fierce battle, the Illusory Bloom's power is destroyed. The Nappo reverts to its original form and flees, hurt and afraid. When you return to Lallipar, you are greeted not by anxiety and chaos, but by an unexpected celebration. The villagers and pilgrims have gathered around a bonfire, preparing a simple yet lively feast to drive away the gloom and welcome back long-lost laughter. Fafala's small gift, Nida's cooking, and the night sky lit by lanterns all make this an unforgettable evening. However, even as joy fills the air, no one can deny it: The Illusory Plague has not gone away. This might just be the brief calm before the storm.
